title: Webshell Upload Detected and Contained: shell.php.jpg

steps.message: Based on the identified 'file uploading' activity and the presence of `shell.php.jpg`, immediately delete the malicious file to prevent further execution.
To prevent further 'file uploading' attempts and other malicious activity, block the source IP address that initiated the webshell upload in the WAF.

params.reason: Webshell upload attempt and file uploading activity

steps.message: Given the potential for wider impact from 'file uploading and downloading' capabilities, initiate an EDR scan on the affected host to detect any additional anomalies or malicious payloads.

Approve the remediation plan to address the root cause of the unrestricted 'file uploading' vulnerability, specifically focusing on reviewing potential 'debug logic' or insecure parameter handling identified in the application.

Following approval, create a development ticket to implement fixes for the 'file uploading' vulnerability, ensuring secure handling and validation, especially concerning any 'hidden parameter discovery' mechanisms.

params.summary: Remediate unrestricted file upload vulnerability and debug logic
params.description: Implement robust validation for file uploads and review potential debug logic or hidden parameters that could bypass security controls, as indicated by 'hidden parameter discovery' methods. This will prevent further 'file uploading' exploits. Assign to IT_ADMIN.

steps.message: Incident response workflow for webshell upload completed. All initial 'anomalies' identified during monitoring are addressed and 'file uploading' controls are being hardened. Further monitoring is advised.

params.message: Webshell upload incident (shell.php.jpg) contained. Remediation ticket (Jira-XXXX) created for IT_ADMIN. Review EDR scan results and ongoing monitoring.
